# Epic Template

```markdown
## Problem Statement
> Single comprehensive sentence/paragraph that answers:
> - Who's struggling? (specific persona or user type)
> - What are they trying to accomplish? (workflow or business outcome)
> - What's going wrong today? (beyond "feature doesn't exist")
> - Do we have quantifiable indicators? (drop-off rates, support tickets, ARR impact, time wasted, etc.)
>
> Example: "Marketing and communications managers are unable to configure clear, meaningful communication preferences for their audiences because preferences are tightly coupled to Projects, forcing unrelated concepts into one system and making email setup confusing and inflexible. This creates friction in campaign setup and introduces revenue risk, impacting customers representing over $410k in ARR."

## Overview
> Brief description of the large initiative and its strategic importance (1-2 sentences)

## Business Context
> Problem statement, current state, and customer impact (3-5 sentences)

## Solution Approach
> High-level description of the proposed solution (3-5 sentences)

## Cross-Feature Requirements
> How this epic integrates with and impacts other platform features
[Include only sections with content - omit N/A sections]

### Person Records
[Details if applicable]

### Segmentation & Targeting
[Details if applicable]

### Reporting & Analytics
[Details if applicable]

### Billing & Credits
[Details if applicable]

### CRM Integration
[Details if applicable]

### Campaign Goals
[Details if applicable]

### Other Platform Touchpoints
[Details if applicable]

## Key Features
> Major features required to complete this epic
- [ ] Feature 1: [Brief description] (#Issue link when created)
- [ ] Feature 2: [Brief description] (#Issue link when created)

## Open Questions
- Design questions:
- Technical questions:
- Business questions:

## Key Milestones
> Major deliverables for tracking progress
- [ ] Milestone 1
- [ ] Milestone 2

## Delivery Strategy
- Rollout approach:
- Feature flag strategy:
- Product tier considerations:
- User type/role access considerations:

## Stakeholders
-
```
